goal: QA audit of sprints 250-251 — verify utility routine correctness, formula edge cases, Magikarp length distribution
abstract_tasks: Verify all home/ utility routines (RNG, math, copy, text, flag, time) behave identically to pokecrystal
Test BattleRandom vs Random separation: BattleRandom deterministic, Random uses DIV entropy
Verify Divide-by-zero behavior: confirm infinite loop on HP<4 switch as documented bug
Test flee formula edge cases: PlayerSpeed >= EnemySpeed always escapes, +30 per failed attempt
Validate Magikarp length distribution: 13-entry threshold table, B-register-only comparison bug reproduced
Audit all formulas in sprint 251: Day Care step EXP gain, Odd Egg shiny rate (14%), egg cycle hatching
Verify Medium-Slow EXP underflow at level 1 is faithfully reproduced (wraps to 16,777,162)

source_data.core_routines.md:
### Divide
`hQuotient = hDividend / hDivisor` (up to 4-byte dividend, 1-byte divisor)
- BUG: Dividing by 0 enters infinite loop (see HP<4 switch bug)

### BattleRandom
Deterministic PRNG for battle engine.
- Uses a 10-byte PRNG stream (wLinkBattleRNCount tracks position)
- Shared seed ensures link battles stay in sync

### Random
Hardware-based RNG using the DIV register (increments at 16384 Hz).
- NOT suitable for link battles (would desync)

### RandomRange
Returns random number in range [0, a).
- Uses rejection sampling to avoid modulo bias
source_data.formulas_reference.md:
## Flee Formula
F = (PlayerSpeed * 32) / (EnemySpeed / 4)
If PlayerSpeed >= EnemySpeed: always escape.
Each failed attempt adds +30 to F.

## Day Care
Each step cycle, Day Care Pokemon gain +1 to their 3-byte experience value.
Cost to retrieve: 100 + (100 * levels_gained)

## Egg Hatching
Each egg species has an 'egg cycles' value. Each cycle = 256 steps. Counter decrements by 1 each cycle.

## Medium Slow EXP BUG
Medium Slow gives negative experience at level 1 due to the formula producing a negative value, causing an underflow to a very large number.

## Happiness Walking
Every 256 steps, a counter toggles. Every other toggle (512 steps effectively), each non-egg party member gains +1 happiness.
source_data.event_data_tables.md:
## Magikarp Length Calculation
The length is calculated from DVs using a threshold table. Due to a bug in the BC comparison, only register B is used for the threshold check.

| BC Threshold | Divisor | Resulting Length Range |
|-------------|---------|----------------------|
| 110 | 1 | Shortest |
| 310 | 2 | Very small |
| 710 | 4 | Small |
| 2,710 | 20 | Below average |
| 7,710 | 50 | Average |
| 17,710 | 100 | Above average |
| 32,710 | 150 | Large |
| 47,710 | 150 | Very large |
| 57,710 | 100 | Huge |
| 62,710 | 50 | Enormous |
| 64,710 | 20 | Near maximum |
| 65,210 | 5 | Exceptional |
| 65,410 | 2 | Near record |

## Odd Egg Shiny Chance: 14% overall (far higher than normal 1/8192)

unstructured_notes: This is the final QA sprint before Phase 16 integration testing begins. Key focus: ensure all utility routines faithfully reproduce pokecrystal's bugs (divide-by-zero, Magikarp B-register-only comparison). The Odd Egg 14% shiny rate must be confirmed. Flee formula must correctly add +30 per failed attempt and short-circuit when PlayerSpeed >= EnemySpeed. The BattleRandom vs Random distinction is critical for link battle sync — verify the 10-byte PRNG stream is used for all battle RNG calls. Medium-Slow EXP underflow must be present since eggs hatch at level 5 partly because of this.

goal: Integration test: New Bark Town through Violet City — full playthrough segment, starter selection, rival battle, Sprout Tower, Gym 1
abstract_tasks: Run headless simulation from game start through Gym 1: clock set, Pokegear received, starter selected, Route 29 traversal
Verify Rival Battle 1 type-advantage logic: rival always receives the starter with type advantage over player choice
Simulate Sprout Tower 7-sage gauntlet; verify HM05 Flash reward from Sage Li and rival sighting cutscene
Test Falkner gym: correct trainer composition (Abe Spearow Lv9, Rod Pidgey Lv7x2), Falkner Pidgey Lv7 + Pidgeotto Lv9

Confirm Togepi Egg delivery from Elm's aide at Violet Pokemon Center post-badge

source_data.game_progression.md:
### Starting Out
1. Game begins in player's bedroom (2F of Player's House)
2. Set time/day
3. Mom gives Pokegear
4. Visit Professor Elm's Lab
5. Choose starter: Chikorita (Grass), Cyndaquil (Fire), or Totodile (Water), all Lv5
6. See rival (Silver) spying through lab window
7. Elm's aide gives 1 Potion
8. Elm asks you to visit Mr. Pokemon on Route 30

### Rival Battle 1
- Silver ambushes you outside Cherrygrove (returning south)
- Silver's team: Stolen starter Lv5 (type advantage over yours)

### Gym 1: Falkner (Flying)
- Gym trainers: Bird Keeper Abe (Spearow Lv9), Bird Keeper Rod (Pidgey Lv7 x2)
- Falkner: Pidgey Lv7, Pidgeotto Lv9
- Zephyr Badge — Flash usable outside battle; Attack boost
- TM31 Mud-Slap
- Elm calls; visit Pokemon Center to receive Mystery Egg from aide (hatches into Togepi)
source_data.gym_strategies.md:
### Gym 1: Falkner (Flying) — Violet City
Falkner's Team:
| Pokemon | Level | Moves |
|---------|-------|-------|
| Pidgey | 7 | Tackle, Mud-Slap, Gust |
| Pidgeotto | 9 | Tackle, Mud-Slap, Gust |
Gym Trainers: Bird Keeper Abe (Spearow Lv9), Bird Keeper Rod (Pidgey Lv7 x2)
Weaknesses: Electric, Rock, Ice
Difficulty: 1/10
source_data.story_events_by_map.md:
### Elm's Lab — Choose Starter
- Flags set: EVENT_GOT_CYNDAQUIL/TOTODILE/CHIKORITA_FROM_ELM, EVENT_GOT_A_POKEMON_FROM_ELM
### Cherrygrove City — Silver Ambush
- Battle: RIVAL1 with stolen starter Lv5 (type advantage over player's choice)
### Violet Pokecenter — Elm's Aide Gives Togepi Egg
- Trigger: Visit Pokecenter after beating Falkner
- Condition: ENGINE_ZEPHYRBADGE set
- Flags set: EVENT_GOT_TOGEPI_EGG_FROM_ELMS_AIDE

unstructured_notes: This is the first true end-to-end integration test. The simulation must run the entire early game as a player would experience it — no shortcuts. Key progression gate: EVENT_GAVE_MYSTERY_EGG_TO_ELM must be set before Route 29 trainers are unlocked and before Elm's aide gives Poke Balls. The rival's type-advantage selection logic must be tested with all three starter choices. Sprout Tower is optional in-game but the simulation should verify it is completable. Falkner's Mud-Slap accuracy reduction must stack correctly if applied multiple times.

source_data.gym_strategies.md:
### Gym 2: Bugsy (Bug) — Azalea Town
Scyther: Fury Cutter doubles damage each consecutive hit (10→20→40→80→160)
Critical: Do NOT let Fury Cutter hit consecutively. Switch Pokemon to reset the counter.

### Gym 3: Whitney (Normal) — Goldenrod City
Key Threats: Miltank's Rollout (doubles each turn, can OHKO after 3-4 hits), Attract (male Pokemon can't attack 50% of time), Milk Drink (heals 50% HP)
Note: Whitney cries after losing. You must talk to the Lass in the gym to receive the badge.
source_data.story_events_by_map.md:
## 9. Azalea Town — Badge 2
[Slowpoke Well scene, Bugsy gym, Rival Battle 2 at Azalea exit, Ilex Forest Farfetch'd puzzle]
Rival Battle 2: Silver appears with Gastly, Zubat, starter evolution
Ilex Forest: Farfetch'd puzzle required before HM01 Cut received

unstructured_notes: Whitney's Miltank is famously the hardest early-game encounter for many players. The integration test must confirm: (1) Rollout damage truly doubles each turn and resets if Miltank uses a different move; (2) Attract 50% immobilize works correctly for male player Pokemon; (3) Milk Drink restores exactly 50% HP and can be used repeatedly; (4) The badge is not given immediately after defeat but only after talking to the Lass. The Fury Cutter doubling chain in Bugsy's gym must also be tested. The Radio Tower quiz hardcoded answer sequence (Yes/No pattern) must produce Radio Card exactly.

Verify save/load round-trip at 5 checkpoints (post-starter, post-rival1, post-gym1, post-gym2, post-gym3)
Confirm Whitney badge gate: game does not award Plain Badge without Lass NPC interaction flag set

source_data.bugs_and_glitches.md:
### 25. Dude Tutorial Crash
The Dude's catching tutorial can crash if party and PC box are both full when it starts.

unstructured_notes: This QA sprint validates the entire early-game integration. The focus is on progression flag ordering (wrong order = wrong game state), the documented Dude Tutorial crash (party + PC full), and the Whitney badge gate (a real softlock if the Lass interaction flag is not respected). Save/load testing at multiple checkpoints ensures serialization correctness for the portion of the game already implemented. The simulation expert should attempt adversarial inputs: KO starter before rival fight, fill PC before Dude tutorial, etc.

source_data.story_events_by_map.md:
## Burned Tower release event — Suicune storyline trigger
Raikou and Entei begin roaming Johto routes after Burned Tower basement fall-through.
Suicune flees then begins its Crystal-specific 6-encounter storyline.

### Lake of Rage — Red Gyarados
- Trigger: Step onto special tile in center of lake
- Red Gyarados Lv30, forced encounter (FORCESHINY battle type)
- Cannot flee, guaranteed shiny DVs (Def 10, Spd 10, Spc 10, Atk 2/3/6/7/10/11/14/15)
source_data.special_encounters.md:
### Raikou and Entei (Roaming)
Both released when you fall through the Burned Tower floor.
They roam Johto routes (grass encounters only).
Each time you enter a new route, they move to a random adjacent route.
They always attempt to flee on the first turn (Roar forces you out too).
HP, status, and catch attempts persist across encounters.
If you KO either one, they disappear permanently.

unstructured_notes: This is the most event-dense integration segment. The Burned Tower floor-collapse must set flags for all three beasts simultaneously. Roaming mechanics (route-changing on map transition, flee-on-first-turn) must be live after this event. The Red Gyarados must use BATTLETYPE_FORCESHINY with the specific guaranteed shiny DV pattern. The Rocket Hideout Electrode switches must be interactable (3 Electrode Lv23 in switches, not standard trainers). Lance appearing at both Lake of Rage and Hideout requires correct scene flag management.

| Pokemon | Level |
|---------|-------|

| Dragonite | 47 |
| Dragonite | 47 |

Pre-E4 Preparation: Recommended Level Range 42-50
source_data.story_events_by_map.md:
### Hall of Fame registration
After defeating Champion Lance, Hall of Fame registration sequence plays.
Post-game state unlocked: Kanto routes accessible via Magnet Train.

unstructured_notes: The E4 gauntlet is the climax of the Johto playthrough. Critical mechanics to verify: no PP/HP restoration between E4 rooms (transition script must not call any heal routine), Koga's Forretress Explosion correctly halves defense then KOs itself, Karen's Gengar Destiny Bond timing (faint KOs attacker on THEIR next move), Lance's 3 Dragonite correct level distribution (47/47/50). The Dragon's Den Elder quiz must branch based on answers to give either ExtremeSpeed Dratini or standard Dratini. Hall of Fame triggers credits and post-game Kanto unlock.

Confirm Red Gyarados shiny DV pattern: Def=10, Spd=10, Spc=10, Atk in {2,3,6,7,10,11,14,15}
Test E4 no-heal: verify full party HP and PP state entering each room matches exit state of previous room
Verify Dragon's Den answer branching: ExtremeSpeed Dratini only on correct-all-answers path

source_data.special_encounters.md:
### Red Gyarados
Level 30. Guaranteed shiny. Cannot flee. FORCESHINY battle type.
Shiny DVs: Def=10, Spd=10, Spc=10, Atk in {2,3,6,7,10,11,14,15}

### Raikou (Roaming)
HP and status persist between encounters.
If KOed, it is gone forever (no respawn).

### Raikou Moves: Roar, Thunder Shock, Reflect, Crunch
### Entei Moves: Roar, Fire Spin, Stomp, Flamethrower

| Pokemon | Level |
|---------|-------|
| Gyarados | 44 |
| Dragonite | 47 |
| Dragonite | 47 |
| Aerodactyl | 46 |
| Dragonite | 50 |
Recommended Level Range: 42-50

unstructured_notes: QA emphasis: the roaming beast HP persistence is a complex state-management requirement — each beast needs its own HP/status tracking that survives save/load cycles. The FORCESHINY battle type for Red Gyarados must override normal DV generation. E4 no-heal requirement means transition scripts must be audited to ensure no heal commands are called. Dragon's Den branch verification requires testing both answer paths. The 'gone forever if KOed' rule for Raikou and Entei must be confirmed with flag-based tracking.

source_data.kanto_map_scripts.md:
### Oak's Lab
- Prof. Oak — Checks badges, evaluates Pokedex. If player has all 16 badges → opens Mt. Silver (EVENT_OPENED_MT_SILVER).
- EVENT_TALKED_TO_OAK_IN_KANTO — Set on first visit

### Viridian Gym
- Blue only appears after EVENT_BLUE_IN_CINNABAR is cleared
- Blue's Dialogue: 'Yo! Finally got here, huh? ...You're telling me you conquered all the GYMS in JOHTO?'

### Pewter City
- Gramps — Gives SILVER_WING (EVENT_GOT_SILVER_WING)
- 'Ah, you came all the way out here from JOHTO? ...Here. I want you to have this item I found in JOHTO.'

source_data.gym_strategies.md:
### Kanto Gym Leaders:
Brock (Rock/Ground) — Geodude Lv41, Rhyhorn Lv41, Omastar Lv42, Kabutops Lv42, Onix Lv44
Misty (Water) — Golduck Lv42, Quagsire Lv42, Lapras Lv44, Starmie Lv47 [approximate]
Blue's team (varied) — mixed powerful team post-Champion

unstructured_notes: The Kanto segment is post-game content unlocked after beating the Johto E4. Key gating: EXPN Card (from Copycat sidequest) needed for Kanto radio, and the Copycat sidequest itself requires finding her Clef Doll in Vermilion. Blue's gym requires talking to him at Cinnabar's rocky beach first. The Silver Wing triggers Lugia access at Whirl Islands. All 16 badges must all be tracked independently. Mt. Silver access is gated on all 16 badges AND talking to Oak — this is the hardest gate to test since it requires the full 200+ hour playthrough simulation.

Easter eggs: MASUDA (Junichi Masuda, composer), TAJIRI (Satoshi Tajiri, Pokemon creator)

source_data.game_progression.md:
### Mt. Silver — exterior, interior floors, summit (Red battle location), high-level wild encounters
Red's team: Pikachu Lv81, full team of 6 at levels 80-84

unstructured_notes: Red's battle is the true final boss. His Pikachu at Lv81 is the highest-level Pikachu in the game and the iconic centerpiece. No dialogue before or after the battle — the silence is canonical. The Battle Tower Crystal 1.0 trainer class bug should be implemented faithfully (6th character of name used as class). The Suicune requirement of 'all 3 Legendary Beasts seen' means the Pokedex must track each beast as 'seen' even if not caught. GS Ball Celebi requires Kurt's one-day crafting timer before shrine placement.

unstructured_notes: Red's exact level values are critical for difficulty calibration — his Pikachu Lv81 is more than 30 levels above the E4 gauntlet's strongest (Lance's Dragonite Lv50), making him a genuine endgame challenge. Battle Tower AI should use the full 8-layer scoring system implemented in Phase 5 sprints. Legendary catch rates must match exactly (3 for all major legendaries, 45 for Celebi). All Suicune encounter triggers in the 6-encounter Crystal chain must have fired before Tin Tower access is enabled.

goal: Integration test: Speedrun route validation — verify any% glitchless route is completable, key split times reasonable
abstract_tasks: Configure simulation with speedrun options: text speed Fast, battle scene Off, battle style Set, clock at 5:50 PM
Run Totodile speedrun route from New Bark to Hall of Fame: verify all mandatory events reachable in optimal order
Verify trainer avoidance is possible: NPC line-of-sight can be bypassed where speedrun route expects it
Simulate key Miltank fight: confirm Rage accumulation mechanic (Attack raises each time hit) and Mud-Slap accuracy reduction interaction with Rollout accuracy
Verify Goldenrod Department Store 5F TM availability: Ice Punch and ThunderPunch purchasable for $3000 each
Confirm speedrun-critical items on path: each required HM, key item, and badge is reachable without detours in stated order

source_data.speedrun_notes.md:
### Key Decisions
- Starter: Totodile (standard route)
- Text Speed: Fast, Battle Scene: Off, Battle Style: Set
- Time: Set to 5:50 PM (specific encounter table manipulation)

### Totodile Stat Requirements
- Need at minimum 10 Special Attack DV (12+ preferred)
- Attack DV should be reasonable (10+)

### Rival Battle 1 Optimal Strategy
Leer first turn is optimal to guarantee 3HKO

### Whitney Segment
- Miltank: Rage (stacks Attack each time hit), then attack. Or use Mud-Slap for accuracy reduction.
- Attract doesn't work if your Totodile is female

### Goldenrod Shopping
- Department Store 5F: Buy TM Ice Punch, TM ThunderPunch ($3000 each)
- Teach Ice Punch and ThunderPunch to Croconaw

### Ilex Forest
- Cut the tree, do Farfetch'd puzzle quickly
- Get HM01 Cut, teach it (or to a slave)
source_data.game_progression.md:
### Route 34 → Goldenrod City
Day Care Center on Route 34 (breeding available)
Get free Bicycle from Bike Shop
Radio Tower: Pass quiz (Yes, Yes, No, Yes, No) for Radio Card
Department Store, Game Corner, Underground Tunnel available

unstructured_notes: The speedrun test validates that all mechanics come together for uninterrupted play. The 5:50 PM start time is a specific encounter table manipulation — the time system must produce the correct table at that hour. Rage's attack accumulation mechanic (each time Miltank hits the Pokemon using Rage, Attack +1 stage) is the key speedrun tech for Whitney. The simulation must confirm NPC sight lines are not triggered when the route intends avoidance. This also stress-tests performance — the simulation should complete New Bark to Hall of Fame without deadlocks or infinite loops.

goal: Integration test: Competitive validation — verify competitive Pokemon are obtainable, movesets learnable, breeding produces correct results, team building viable
abstract_tasks: Verify all GSC OU Ubers and S-tier Pokemon are obtainable in-game: Snorlax (trade-only in Crystal, confirmed in FAQ), Raikou, Zapdos, Tyranitar, Skarmory, Suicune
Test Snorlax's Curse + RestTalk moveset is achievable via TM/level-up combination and that it holds Leftovers correctly
Verify Spikes mechanics: only Cloyster and Forretress learn Spikes; 3 layers deal 1/8, 1/6, 1/4 HP on switch-in; Rapid Spin from Starmie/Forretress removes all layers
Simulate breeding edge case: Heracross with Megahorn from level-up, confirm TM compatibility for Cross Chop (Machamp)
Test Hidden Power type formula for competitive DVs: verify type derivation from AtkDV and DefDV low bits, power derivation from high bits
Verify Thick Club Marowak overflow bug is reproducible (Swords Dance + Thick Club at high level wraps Attack to tiny value)

source_data.competitive_gen2.md:
## GSC OU Key Mechanics

### Spikes
- Only Cloyster and Forretress learn Spikes
- Up to 3 layers: 1/8, 1/6, 1/4 of max HP on switch-in
- Rapid Spin removes Spikes (Starmie, Forretress, Cloyster are key spinners)
- Spikes + phazing (Roar/Whirlwind) is the primary strategy

### S Tier: Snorlax
Curse sweeper, mixed attacker, special wall. ~95% usage rate.

### A+ Tier: Raikou
Premier special wall, best Spikes shuffler, Sleep Talk + Roar

### A Tier: Forretress
Spikes + Rapid Spin (only Pokemon with both), Explosion

## Critical Hits in Gen 2
Focus Energy + Scope Lens can increase rate significantly.
Critical hits in Gen 2 ignore negative stat changes on attacker AND positive stat changes on defender
source_data.team_building.md:
### Key Types to Have on Your Team
1. Water (Surf is required HM)
2. Electric (covers Water, Flying) — Ampharos (Mareep from Route 32)
3. Fighting or Ground
4. Ice (covers Dragon) — Lapras, Piloswine, Ice Beam TM

unstructured_notes: Snorlax is trade-only in Crystal (confirmed in FAQ: 'There is no wild Snorlax in Crystal. You must trade from Red/Blue/Yellow or Gold/Silver.') — the test must confirm the trading/Time Capsule pathway works for it. Hidden Power type and power formulas must be implemented exactly — competitive players use specific DVs to achieve desired Hidden Power types. The Thick Club overflow bug is not a casual concern but matters for competitive accuracy. Spikes layer tracking (3 separate HP thresholds) must be verified with switch-in testing.

goal: Final QA audit — complete game regression, 205 QA pairs verification, all 81 data files consumed, softlock sweep, save corruption testing
abstract_tasks: Run all 205 QA pairs from qa_pairs.md as automated factual checks against the live engine (species stats, move data, item prices, gym teams, mechanic descriptions)
Execute full softlock sweep: attempt all known crash/freeze conditions (divide-by-zero switch bug, Dude tutorial full-box crash, party wipe to poison in overworld)
Save corruption test battery: save at 20 checkpoints, corrupt 1 byte each, verify graceful error handling
Verify all 81 data files have corresponding engine coverage: cross-check master coverage list against implemented modules
Run 50 random battle scenarios from battle_scenarios.md and verify damage, status, and outcome match expected values from pokecrystal
Final bug fidelity audit: confirm all 92 documented bugs are either faithfully reproduced with ASM citation or explicitly noted as intentional deviations with justification

source_data.faq.md:
### Q: How do I get Snorlax?
There is no wild Snorlax in Crystal. Trade from Red/Blue/Yellow or Gold/Silver.

### Q: How do I catch Suicune in Pokemon Crystal?
Suicune has a fixed encounter in Crystal. After encountering it at multiple locations, it waits at Tin Tower Basement (Lv40). Needs Clear Bell.

### Q: How do I get Dratini?
(1) Dragon's Den with ExtremeSpeed if correct answers given. (2) Fish in Dragon's Den. (3) Game Corner 2100 coins.
source_data.bugs_and_glitches.md:
### 20. Switch Freeze with Max HP < 4
Switching calculates enemy HP percentage using max_HP / 4 as denominator. If max HP < 4, divides by zero, entering an infinite loop.

### 25. Dude Tutorial Crash
Can crash if party and PC box are both full when it starts.

### 26. BRN/PSN/PAR Don't Affect Catch Rate
Code checks for FRZ/SLP but skips BRN/PSN/PAR check. Only Sleep (+10) and Freeze (+10) work.

### 6. Belly Drum HP Glitch
Belly Drum calls AttackUp2 BEFORE checking if user has 50% HP.

unstructured_notes: This is the final sprint of the entire 264-sprint sequence. The goal is comprehensive validation rather than new feature implementation. The 205 QA pairs provide a structured regression harness. Key final checks: (1) All 81 data files in the master coverage list have corresponding engine modules; (2) The bug fidelity audit must produce a documented decision for each of the 92 bugs — reproduce or intentionally skip with written justification; (3) Save corruption handling is essential for a shipped product — the engine must not panic on malformed save data. The simulation expert should run the most adversarial tests of the entire project. This sprint represents the completion of the Crusty Pokemon Crystal clone engine.
